pages.text: Sharpening adds $(thing)charges$() to axes, picks, shovels, swords, and other sharpenable tools. Charges boost $(thing)mining speed$() while digging and add $(thing)flat damage$() on the next hit — then consume one charge.$(br2)Three tiers of sharpener determine the charge cap and power.

$(thing)Whetstones$() are the simplest sharpener. Hold in one hand, sharpenable tool in the other, then right-click. Each use takes one durability from the whetstone. Caps at 64 charges ($(#008000)Tier 1$()).
Forge a honing steel head from steel.
$(thing)Honing Steel$() — assemble from head and two wooden rods. Faster than a whetstone, adds more charges per use, and caps at 128 charges ($(#0000AA)Tier 2$()). Takes durability per use.
Weld together the diamond honing steel head.
$(thing)Diamond Honing Steel$() — the most powerful sharpener. Adds the most charges per use, caps at 256 charges ($(#550055)Tier 3$()), and is nearly instant. Takes durability per use.

pages.title: Sharpness Tiers
pages.text: $(li)$(#008000)Tier 1$() (1–64 charges): +3× mining speed, +1 weapon damage$(li)$(#0000AA)Tier 2$() (65–128 charges): +5× mining speed, +2 weapon damage$(li)$(#550055)Tier 3$() (129–256 charges): +7× mining speed, +3 weapon damage$(br2)Charge count is shown in the item tooltip.
The $(thing)Grindstone$() block is an automated sharpening station. Craft the base frame from iron or steel rods and lumber.
$(thing)Quartz Wheel$() — the basic grindstone wheel. Crafted from quartzite blocks around an axle. Corresponds to Tier 1 sharpening.
$(thing)Steel Wheel$() — weld a Quartz Wheel with a Black Steel double sheet. Corresponds to Tier 2 sharpening.
$(thing)Diamond Wheel$() — surround a Steel Wheel with diamond powder. Corresponds to Tier 3 sharpening.

pages.title: Using the Grindstone
pages.text: Right-click the block to insert a $(thing)wheel item$(), then insert a sharpenable tool. Connect a TFC axle to either of the two sides $(italic)perpendicular to the wheel face$(). Sufficient axle speed is required to operate — faster rotation means faster sharpening progress.
